name: linear-projects-write
description: Create and update Linear projects via CLI (write operations)
capabilities: Create new Linear projects with name, teams, and description
Update existing projects with new name, description, lead, or status
Set project dates including start date and target date
Configure project priority levels from urgent to low
Support partial name matching for project identification
Output results in JSON format for automation
author: Bind
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill definition containing no executable source code. All 65 static findings are false positives caused by the analyzer misinterpreting documentation examples, version numbers, and environment variable references as security threats. The skill consists of SKILL.md (CLI documentation) and skill.json (configuration), referencing source files that do not exist in this registry. The previous AI audit correctly classified this as safe.
valueStatement: Managing Linear projects manually takes time and can lead to inconsistent project setups. This skill provides simple CLI commands to create and update Linear projects directly from your terminal with standardized options for teams, leads, dates, and priorities.
limitations: Requires LINEAR_API_KEY environment variable to be set
Source files not included in this registry definition
User names must exist in Linear to resolve project leads
Bun runtime must be installed to execute commands

useCases.title: Quick project setup
useCases.description: Create new feature branches and projects in Linear with one command

useCases.title: Standardize project workflows
useCases.description: Ensure all new projects follow consistent naming and priority conventions

useCases.title: Automate project creation
useCases.description: Integrate project creation into CI/CD pipelines for automated sprint setup

promptTemplates.title: Create simple project
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Linear project called [Project Name] for the [Team Name] team

promptTemplates.title: Create project with details
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new project with name [Name], teams [Team1,Team2], description [Description], lead [Lead Name], start date [YYYY-MM-DD], target date [YYYY-MM-DD], and priority [Priority Level]

promptTemplates.title: Update project status
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the [Project Name] project to show status [New Status] and target date [YYYY-MM-DD]

promptTemplates.title: Full project update
promptTemplates.prompt: Update project [Project Identifier] with new name [Name], description [Description], lead [Lead Name or none], status [Status], start date [YYYY-MM-DD], target date [YYYY-MM-DD], and priority [0-4]

bestPractices: Store LINEAR_API_KEY securely in environment variables or .env files
Use consistent naming conventions across all projects for easier searching
Set target dates during project creation to track delivery timelines
Add clear descriptions to help team members understand project scope
antiPatterns: Creating projects without descriptions makes tracking purpose difficult
Skipping team assignment limits visibility and access control
Using inconsistent naming conventions breaks partial name matching
Ignoring priority settings leads to unclear work prioritization

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install the skill and use Linear commands in your natural language prompts.

faq.question: How many projects can I create or update?
faq.answer: The skill uses Linear API rate limits. For bulk operations, implement delays between requests to avoid hitting limits.

faq.question: Does this integrate with existing Linear workspaces?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill connects to any Linear workspace where your API key has access. Team and user permissions apply.

faq.question: Is my Linear data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill only accesses Linear through authenticated API calls. Your API key is never stored or transmitted beyond Linear API endpoints.

faq.question: Why is my project not found during updates?
faq.answer: Verify the project name or ID is correct. The skill supports partial name matching but case sensitivity applies. Check that you have access to the project.

faq.question: How does this compare to Linear web interface?
faq.answer: This skill enables faster project creation through automation and templates. The web interface offers more configuration options and visual project boards.
